topic.prompt_en: Describe a place you enjoy spending time in.
topic.learning_goal: Learn to describe a familiar place through atmosphere, activity, one scene, and personal connection.

angles.title: Identify the place
content: Say where it is and how it fits into your life.
Choose a place you know well enough to describe naturally.

angles.title: Build the atmosphere
content: Mention sound, light, pace, or people to make the place vivid.
Do not list too many visual details without purpose.

angles.title: Show what you do there
content: Connect the place to one repeated activity such as reading, walking, or thinking.
This gives the answer motion instead of static description.

angles.title: End with meaning
content: Explain what the place gives you emotionally or mentally.
The meaning can be calm, focus, comfort, or perspective.

sequence.target: Introduce the place clearly and explain when you usually go there.
Describe what it feels like and what you usually do there.
Say why you keep returning to this place.

high_score_tips: Use sensory detail only when it supports mood or habit.
Places become memorable when you connect them to a recurring action.
Keep the location familiar and realistic instead of trying to sound exotic.
A calm place can still be interesting if the reflection is specific.

content.why_it_hurts: The answer sounds detached because it focuses on facts instead of experience.
content.fix: Describe what you do there and how the place affects you.

content.why_it_hurts: The place feels vague if the listener cannot imagine the mood.
content.fix: Use two small details such as morning light, quiet paths, or slow foot traffic.

content.why_it_hurts: That ending is too flat for a full 1-2 minute response.
content.fix: Say what the place helps you do or feel, such as reset, focus, or think.

content.why_it_hurts: The response sounds memorised if it stays at the level of general ideas and never lands on one lived detail.
content.fix: Add one concrete moment, reaction, or small consequence that actually happened to you.

content.problem: Reflection that only repeats the opening
content.why_it_hurts: The ending feels flat if it only restates the topic instead of adding insight or change.
content.fix: Use the final lines to show what changed, what you realised, or how you would respond now.

language.wrong_example: There have many trees and people.
language.better_example: There are lots of trees, and the area is usually quite peaceful.
language.reason: There are is the correct structure for describing what exists in a place.

language.wrong_example: It is beautiful and the view is beautiful too.
language.better_example: It is peaceful, open, and especially pleasant in the early evening.
language.reason: Mood words often sound more natural than repeating beautiful.

language.problem: Overusing very or really with basic adjectives
language.wrong_example: It was very good and really important for me.
language.better_example: It turned out to be more meaningful than I expected.
language.reason: A precise evaluation sounds more natural than stacking intensifiers on basic adjectives.

language.problem: Repeating I think in every sentence
language.wrong_example: I think it is useful, and I think it matters because I think it affects daily life.
language.better_example: What makes it useful to me is the way it affects daily life.
language.reason: Removing repeated I think makes the answer sound more confident and more fluent.

core.example: It is one of the most peaceful places near my apartment.
It is a small green space in the middle of a busy area.
There is a simple pathway that goes around the park.
I usually sit on a bench near the water.
It is the kind of place where I can unwind after work.
The park is nearby, so it is easy to visit regularly.

extended.note: Useful for parks, neighborhoods, or calm public spaces.
Good when a place helps you breathe or think more clearly.
A strong reflective phrase for places you visit after work.
Natural spoken English for small, less obvious places.
Useful for describing a place that is busy enough to feel safe but not crowded.
Very common and natural in spoken English.

core.phrase: go there on a regular basis
core.example: I go there on a regular basis, especially after work.
I usually take a walk there before dinner.
Sometimes I just sit and people-watch for a while.
It is my go-to place when I need to clear my head.
Going there helps me step away from the noise of the city.
I usually feel instantly calmer when I arrive.

extended.note: Useful for describing hidden or quiet places in cities.
extended.example: It is tucked away behind a row of apartment buildings.

extended.phrase: give me a sense of space
extended.note: Good for parks, river walks, and open public areas.
extended.example: The open view gives me a sense of space.

extended.phrase: reset before the next part of the day
extended.meaning_zh: 在下一阶段开始前重置状态
extended.note: Strong reflective phrase for routine places.
extended.example: It helps me reset before the next part of the day.

extended.note: Useful when the place is ordinary but personally meaningful.
extended.example: It is not impressive on paper, but it matters a lot to me.

framework.goal: Describe a place through mood, routine, and meaning rather than facts alone.

moves: Say what the place is and where it is.
Mention when you usually go there.
Describe the atmosphere with two useful details.
Explain what you normally do there.
End with one clear emotional or mental effect.

delivery_markers: What I like most about this place is ...
It is not famous or anything, but ...
That is why I keep going back there.

samples.answer:
The place I want to describe is a small riverside park near my apartment. It is only about a ten-minute walk away, so I go there quite often, especially in the evening.

It is not a very large or famous place, but it is clean, peaceful, and full of trees. There is also a walking path and a few benches near the water. After work, I usually go there to take a short walk or just sit down for a while. The atmosphere is much calmer than the main streets nearby.

One reason I like it is that it helps me relax and clear my head. If I stay at home all day, I feel tired and stuck. But when I spend even twenty minutes in that park, I feel better. So it is a simple place, but it has become important to my routine.
highlights: Simple sensory detail with routine use
Clear reason for returning to the place

opening: The place I want to describe is ...
body: It is not very large or famous, but ...
closing: It helps me relax and clear my head.

upgrades.improved: It is peaceful and much calmer than the streets nearby.
upgrades.why: Mood-based detail sounds more natural than generic praise.

upgrades.improved: It has become important to my routine.
upgrades.why: The effect on daily life gives the answer more weight.

upgrades.original: It was very important to me.
upgrades.improved: What made it important was the effect it had on the way I handled similar situations later.
upgrades.why: This explains importance through a concrete effect instead of a vague adjective.
upgrades.note: Useful when you want a simple but natural reflective sentence.

upgrades.original: I learned a lot from it.
upgrades.improved: I came away from it with a clearer idea of what I would do next time.
upgrades.why: This sounds more spoken and more specific than a flat learned a lot.
upgrades.note: Good for endings that focus on takeaway and future behaviour.

samples.answer:
I would like to talk about a small riverside park in my neighborhood that I visit several times a week. It is tucked away behind a row of apartment buildings, so people outside the area usually do not even know it exists.

What makes it special to me is not that it is visually impressive, but that it feels slow-paced and open compared with the rest of the city. There are trees along the pathway, a few benches facing the water, and usually only light foot traffic. I often go there after work to walk for twenty or thirty minutes, or sometimes just to sit quietly and think.

A small routine like that makes a bigger difference than it sounds. The park gives me a sense of space and helps me mentally reset before I go home. That is why, despite being such an ordinary local spot, it is probably my favorite place to spend time in.
highlights: Better control of atmosphere and reflection
Uses tucked away and mentally reset naturally

opening: I would like to talk about ...
body: What makes it special to me is not ... but ...
I often go there after work ...
closing: That is why ... it is probably my favorite place ...

upgrades.improved: It feels slow-paced and open compared with the rest of the city.
upgrades.why: This sounds more layered than a single adjective.

upgrades.improved: It helps me mentally reset before I go home.
upgrades.why: Mentally reset gives a more specific emotional effect.

upgrades.improved: What helped most was that it changed the way I judged the situation in practice.
upgrades.why: This sounds more mature and more natural than the vague phrase helped me a lot.
upgrades.note: Useful when you want to show growth without sounding dramatic.

upgrades.improved: It gave me a more balanced way to look at similar situations afterwards.
upgrades.why: Balanced way to look at is a more idiomatic reflective phrase in spoken English.
upgrades.note: Works well in opinion, process, and experience answers.

samples.answer:
The place I would like to describe is a small riverside park near my apartment, and what I appreciate about it is precisely its lack of spectacle. It is not a landmark, it is not particularly fashionable, and yet it has become one of the few places in my week where I genuinely slow down.

Physically, it is quite simple: a narrow path, a line of trees, a few benches, and a broad view of the water. However, the atmosphere is what keeps drawing me back. Because the foot traffic is light and the space feels slightly removed from the surrounding streets, it gives me the impression that the day has briefly loosened its grip on me.

I usually go there after work, especially when my head feels crowded. Even a short walk there helps me sort out my thoughts and return home in a better frame of mind. In that sense, the park is less a destination and more a reliable pause in the middle of an otherwise fast-moving routine.
highlights: Band 8 answer uses rhythm and restraint instead of difficult content
Reliable pause is a strong reflective closing

opening: What I appreciate about it is precisely ...
It has become one of the few places ...
body: However, the atmosphere is what keeps drawing me back.
closing: In that sense, the park is less a destination and more ...

upgrades.improved: It gives me a reliable pause in the middle of an otherwise fast-moving routine.
upgrades.why: This sounds more reflective while staying natural.
upgrades.note: Good for places that create balance.

upgrades.improved: It feels slightly removed from the surrounding streets.
upgrades.why: Concrete atmosphere often sounds stronger than direct labels.
upgrades.note: Band 8 answers often imply mood rather than naming it directly.

upgrades.improved: What stayed with me was not the event itself, but the shift it created in my judgment afterwards.
upgrades.why: This upgrade sounds more nuanced and more reflective than a broad claim like it changed me.
upgrades.note: Strong for higher-band closings that need subtlety rather than drama.

upgrades.improved: Its real value lay in the way it quietly reshaped my response patterns over time.
upgrades.why: Quietly reshaped and over time make the reflection sound more precise and more natural.
upgrades.note: Useful when you want a sophisticated closing without sounding academic.

practice.task: Describe a place you enjoy spending time in and explain why it has become meaningful to you.
checklist: Say where the place is and when you go there.
Describe the atmosphere with useful detail.
Explain what you do there and why you return.
self_prompts: What does it feel like there?
What do you usually do there?
How does the place affect your mood?
